texto: Mom rubbed the back of my neck and we kept walking, away from the kids and the colors and the high-pitched, happy voices. Seeing them made me feel like I was a million miles from anything good. I just got really lonely. I'm not sure why. All those kids smiling and laughing and my mom so fucking clueless and me feeling kinda shitty and high at the same time. All of a sudden, I couldn't figure out what the point was. I couldn't remember what mattered.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amy Sargent Swank: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Mom rubbed the back of my neck and we kept walking, away from the kids and the colors and the hig…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, addiction, addiction-and-recovery pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de addiction-and-recovery e literary-fiction.
